mnemonics.title: BIOS Boot Order
mnemonics.content: BIOS Order: POST → Load MBR → Find Boot Sector → Execute OS Loader. Think: 'Please Open My Fresh Smoothie' (POST → Open → MBR → Find → Sector)

mnemonics.title: UEFI Advantages
mnemonics.content: UEFI benefits: FAGS (Fast, GPT support, GUI, Secure boot). Remember: 'Fast And Great Security'

mnemonics.title: Bootloader Stages
mnemonics.content: Stage 1 (ROM) → Stage 2 (Flash) → OS Load. Think: '1st Floor (ROM) → 2nd Floor (Flash) → Penthouse (OS)'

mnemonics.title: Firmware Properties
mnemonics.content: FIRMWARE: Fixed in ROM, Inmutable (usually), Resides in non-volatile memory, Works at low level, Always needed for boot, Memory efficient, Aware of hardware, Relies on specific device, Enters before OS, Doesn't change often. Remember: 'FIRMWARE Stands For Fixed In ROM, Works At Low Level'

acronyms.usage: Traditional firmware that initializes hardware during boot on older systems
Modern firmware interface replacing BIOS with better security and faster boot
Diagnostic test performed by firmware to check hardware health
Modern partitioning scheme supported by UEFI for disks over 2TB
Traditional 512-byte boot sector containing bootloader and partition table
Communication protocol used to access flash memory storing firmware
Standard interface for debugging and programming microcontrollers
Error-checking method used to verify firmware integrity
Universal Boot Loader - open source bootloader for embedded systems

visualTips.visualization: Draw a vertical flowchart: Power ON → ROM (First-stage bootloader) → Flash Memory (Second-stage) → OS Loader → Operating System. Use arrows showing progression from bottom to top.
Create a two-column comparison table. Left: BIOS (16-bit, legacy, no secure boot). Right: UEFI (64-bit, modern, secure boot, GUI). Circle the key differences.
Draw memory map: ROM at bottom (0x0000), then Flash, then RAM. Show bootloader executing from ROM then moving to RAM. Label each section clearly.
Draw 512-byte box: First 446 bytes = bootstrap code, Bytes 447-510 = partition table (4 entries × 16 bytes), Bytes 511-510 = 0x55AA (boot signature). Color-code each section.

analogies.analogy: Firmware is like the BIOS chip in a car - it's pre-programmed instructions that tell the car engine how to start and operate before the driver takes control.
Bootloader is like a building's security guard who checks if everything is safe (POST), then directs you to the elevator (OS loader) that takes you to your floor (operating system).
UEFI replacing BIOS is like upgrading from a text-based phone menu (BIOS) to a smartphone touchscreen interface (UEFI) - more features, faster, and more secure.
Multi-stage bootloader is like relay race - first runner (ROM bootloader) passes the baton (execution) to second runner (flash bootloader) who then hands to final runner (OS).
